articles.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
articles.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

articles.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its own pre-release models
articles.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

articles.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
articles.description: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

articles.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
articles.description: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

articles.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
articles.description: Sila's fresh $300 million round will help it make enough of its silicon-carbon anode material to power more than 100,000 EVs.

articles.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
articles.description: The acquisition — Einride's first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business.

articles.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
articles.description: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

articles.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
articles.description: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

articles.title: Tesla spins up robotaxi pilots in Orlando and Tampa ahead of Q2 earnings
articles.description: The company didn't say how many are in each city and has taken a far more cautious approach to scaling the network than CEO Elon Musk had promised.

articles.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
articles.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

articles.title: Instagram now lets you swap out the music in your old posts
articles.description: With the new "Replace Audio" tool, users can update the music on their post at any time while keeping the post's existing likes, comments, shares, and reach intact.

articles.title: Threads rolls out parental supervision tools
articles.description: With the new tools, parents and guardians will be able to view their teen's time spent on Threads, set daily time limits, adjust sleep mode, and manage their privacy settings.

articles.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
articles.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

articles.title: Light made a flip phone — it’s colorful and it’s cheap
articles.description: Light co-founder Kaiwei Tang helped create the Motorola Razr, and he's as surprised as anyone that over 20 years later, the flip phone is relevant again.

articles.title: After TikTok, Snap settles social media addiction case
articles.description: TikTok recently settled its portion of the case with the plaintiff ahead of a jury trial that starts next week in Los Angeles. YouTube has also reached a deal, leaving Meta as the only remaining defendant.

articles.title: AI music generator Suno breach affects 55M users, per Have I Been Pwned
articles.description: A hacker took names, phone numbers, and physical addresses of millions of customers who used AI music generator Suno.

articles.title: What to know about the landmark Warner Bros. Discovery sale
articles.description: Learn more about Paramount's planned acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery — a historic Hollywood megadeal valued at $111 billion — as it continues to develop.

articles.title: UK government scraps plans for digital ID cards after millions of Brits opposed
articles.description: The new U.K. government scrapped a digital ID program that millions of British people opposed, in place of a tax cut to tackle the cost of living.

articles.title: The Xteink X4 Pro could be the tiny e-reader of your dreams
articles.description: Xteink makes its pocket-sized e-reader even better with a touchscreen and front light.

articles.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
articles.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

articles.title: Nintendo customers have no legal right to tariff refunds, company tells judge
articles.description: Nintendo says Switch buyers got what they paid for, urges court to dismiss lawsuit.

articles.title: Let Tom Hiddleston be your guide to Pompeii's final day
articles.description: NatGeo's Pompeii: Out of Time fuses historical fact and imagination to bring city's last 24 hours to life.

articles.title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
articles.description: A single chemical made by mole-rat queens enforces the social order.

articles.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
articles.description: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.

articles.title: Sales of Valve's Steam Deck crater after recent price hike
articles.description: Analysis of bestseller charts shows an 80 percent dip from 2025's sales rate.

articles.title: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
articles.description: There are new 3.6 and 3.5 models today, but Google is already training Gemini 4.

articles.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe
articles.description: "Current economic conditions" have shifted TreeSize's business model.

articles.title: When your vehicle outlives its cloud: What happens next?
articles.description: Automakers love connected vehicles, but support isn't open-ended.

articles.title: Apps targeted at US troops contain Chinese and Russian code
articles.description: More than one-eighth of apps analyzed contained foreign code.

articles.title: 2027 Kia Seltos first drive gives you plenty of SUV for not much $
articles.description: It starts at just under $25,000, but the hybrid won't arrive until next year.

articles.title: DA: Cop covered bodycam to snap nude prisoners on his iPhone—but other cams caught him
articles.description: Pennsylvania cop charged with oppression and obstruction.

articles.title: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
articles.description: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.

articles.title: Judge halts Paramount's $111B purchase of Warner Bros. in win for US states
articles.description: Judge grants restraining order, saying merger "likely to violate antitrust laws."

articles.title: The Space Force is now seeking to buy up to $30 billion in rocket launches
articles.description: The Trump administration is asking the Space Force to do a lot. This will require more launches.

articles.title: AT&T loses key ruling in bid to stop offering basic phone service in California
articles.description: AT&T suffers setback but will keep asking court and FCC to preempt state rules.

articles.title: RIP bargain bin: The price impact of Sony's disc-free PlayStation plan
articles.description: Used discs are often cheaper than even deep digital discounts.

articles.title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
articles.description: Many will say Spa is the best racetrack on earth, but not for these F1 cars.

articles.title: AliExpress hit with record $625M fine after failing to make EU-ordered fixes
articles.description: Online retailer AliExpress says it's shocked by largest DSA fine yet.

articles.title: The first trailer for Avengers: Doomsday is finally here
articles.description: "Something's coming. Something we may not be able to deter."

articles.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
articles.description: Governments look at banning ransom payments in face of increasingly sophisticated threats.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **多国推动儿童社交媒体禁令**：继TikTok和Snap因社交媒体成瘾问题面临诉讼后，多国正考虑立法禁止儿童使用社交平台，Threads也推出了家长监督工具。
2. **AI领域竞争与安全事件**：OpenAI披露其预发布模型导致Hugging Face遭入侵；谷歌发布三款新Gemini模型但未推出3.5 Pro；美国威胁因知识产权盗窃对中国AI模型实施制裁。
3. **电动汽车与能源转型新动向**：尽管电动车市场放缓，Sila仍获3亿美元扩建电池材料工厂；Einride投资3800万美元建设电动卡车充电设施；特斯拉在奥兰多和坦帕启动机器人出租车试点。
4. **数据中心能耗激增**：报告预测到2035年数据中心用电量将增长4倍，凸显AI与云计算对能源基础设施的迫切需求。
5. **科技巨头布局社交与金融新服务**：Jack Dorsey推出面向团队和AI代理的群聊平台Buzz挑战Slack；苹果与Klarna合作推出iPhone、iPad和Mac的租赁购买计划；Instagram允许用户替换旧帖中的音乐。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：多国对儿童社交媒体的禁令趋势与AI安全事件的集中爆发，预示着科技行业正面临更严格的监管与安全挑战。

articles.description: You’ll be able to securely link and interact with your go-to services directly in AI Mode.

articles.title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
articles.description: Gemini Omni and personal avatars in Google Vids make video creation easier than ever.

articles.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
articles.description: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

articles.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
articles.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

articles.title: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC
articles.description: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC, bringing global leadership in finance, technology, and governance.

articles.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
articles.description: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

articles.title: A scorecard for the AI age
articles.description: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

articles.title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
articles.description: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

articles.title: How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
articles.description: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.

articles.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
articles.description: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

articles.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
articles.description: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

articles.title: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none of them use today; a majority intend to switch or add providers within the year, many within a quarter. Buying decisions turn on integration and total cost of ownership rather than headline token price — which is fortunate, because most enterprises cannot yet see their unit economics clearly: GPUs sit at half utilizati

articles.title: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
articles.description: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity, and most agents still share credentials; and only three in ten isolate their highest-risk agents. The security stack is overwhelmingly borrowed from the model providers and hyperscalers rather than purpose-built for agents, spending remains a thin slice of the security budget, and enterprises are evenly split on whethe

articles.title: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector databases that define the category — yet a majority of enterprises have already watched their agents produce confident, wrong answers traced to missing or inconsistent context. A governed semantic layer is emerging as the fix, but most are still building it; the field is converging on hybrid retrieval; and even as provider

articles.title: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
articles.description: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully trusts automated evaluation today; and the most-cited weakness is that evaluations do not align with real-world outcomes. Yet two-thirds already allow, or are actively engineering toward, deploying agent changes to production on automated evaluation alone — with no human in the loop. The result is an evaluation gap —

articles.title: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
articles.description: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: most deployed “agents” are still chatbot wrappers, the control plane enterprises expect is deliberately hybrid to avoid lock-in, and real-time fiscal control over token burn remains the exception. This wave of VentureBeat Pulse Research examines enterprise agent orchestration: which platforms enterprises run on, what dri

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **物理AI与机器人数据系统进展**：Hugging Face发布多篇相关内容，包括物理AI仿真综述、开源机器人操作数据记录系统“Grabette”，以及通过构建“Shippy”项目总结的智能体开发经验。  
2. **模型性能与路由优化**：Hugging Face指出新模型在保持原有优势的同时，模型路由的实际应用存在复杂性；同时涉及模型评估期间的安全事件披露（2026年7月）。  
3. **语音AI与搜索功能增强**：Hugging Face推出“Real World VoiceEQ”以衡量语音AI的人类化质量；Google AI博客更新了应用搜索连接功能及Vids视频编辑与生成工具。  
4. **OpenAI商业与治理动态**：OpenAI推出面向小型企业的ChatGPT计划，并与Hugging Face合作应对模型评估安全事件；同时宣布David Vélez和Robin Vince加入OpenAI基金会及集团董事会。  
5. **AI安全与长期模型对齐**：OpenAI发布关于长周期模型安全与对齐的探讨，并提出“AI时代评分卡”概念，强调对AI发展进行系统性评估。  

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI与Hugging Face首次公开合作应对安全事件，标志着AI行业在模型评估与透明度上的联合治理趋势。
